quiz.title: Midweek Quiz: Units 1 & 2
quiz.description:
This quiz covers material from **Units 1 and 2** (Days 1–5): ocean geography, seawater chemistry, phytoplankton and primary production, and zooplankton and food webs.
You have **30 minutes** and **2 attempts** — your highest score will be kept.

questions.question:
The Southern Ocean is sometimes considered Earth's fifth ocean basin.
True False

Which process drives seafloor spreading at mid-ocean ridges?
Subduction of oceanic crust beneath continental crust Magma upwelling from the mantle creating new oceanic crust Erosion of continental shelf sediments Glacial isostatic rebound

Ocean acidification occurs primarily because the ocean absorbs excess atmospheric:
Nitrogen (N₂) Methane (CH₄) Carbon dioxide (CO₂) Sulfur dioxide (SO₂)

Phytoplankton produce roughly 50% of Earth's oxygen through photosynthesis.
True False

Which factor most directly triggers the spring phytoplankton bloom in temperate seas?
A sudden increase in ocean temperature Increased daylight hours combined with shallow mixed-layer depth allowing nutrient-rich water near the surface Upwelling driven by seasonal winds Migration of zooplankton away from surface waters

The "biological pump" refers to:
Mechanical mixing of ocean layers by wind Upwelling of deep, nutrient-rich water to the surface The transport of carbon from the surface ocean to the deep sea through sinking organic matter Filtration of seawater by whale sharks and manta rays

Copepods are considered the most [BLANK1] animals on Earth and serve as a critical link between [BLANK2] and higher trophic levels.
abundant
phytoplankton primary producers

Explain how thermohaline circulation (the "ocean conveyor belt") is driven by differences in water density. In your answer, identify at least two factors that affect seawater density.
thermohaline circulation is driven by differences in temperature and salinity which affect density; cold salty water sinks in polar regions driving deep water formation and global circulation
